mnemonics: P.U.S.H. (Performance Up, Seconds Hold) - To remember that higher clock frequency (more pulses) increases performance, measured in seconds (time) per task.
keyPoints: Clock frequency (Hz) determines how many cycles a processor completes per second.
Cycle time is the duration of one clock cycle, the reciprocal of frequency (T = 1/f).
CPU Time = (CPU Clock Cycles) * (Cycle Time); directly relates program performance to clock speed.
commonMistakes: Assuming a doubling of clock speed always halves execution time (ignores changes in CPI or instruction count).
Equating a higher clock rate directly with a 'better' processor (ignoring other architectural factors like IPC).
